prompts.title: EIA Executive Summary Drafting
prompts.prompt: Act as an EIA consultant accredited by NABET / QCI with 11 years across Indian projects. Draft a 1,500-word executive summary for an EIA report on a 110 MW hydroelectric project in Himachal Pradesh. Cover: (1) project description, (2) baseline environment (air, water, soil, biodiversity, socio-economic), (3) impact identification + significance matrix, (4) mitigation measures, (5) environmental monitoring plan, (6) cost of environmental safeguards, (7) public consultation summary, (8) EMP budget, (9) compliance with EIA Notification 2006 (and 2024 amendments). Constraints: cite IS standards + CPCB norms; 12 affected gram panchayats consulted; no greenwash claims; bilingual (Hindi + English) appendix.

prompts.title: Air Quality Data Interpretation
prompts.prompt: Act as a CPCB-empanelled air-quality scientist with 9 years. I will paste 30-day continuous PM2.5 + PM10 + NO2 + SO2 + O3 + CO data from a CAAQMS in Delhi (Anand Vihar). Provide: (1) descriptive statistics + AQI category split, (2) diurnal + weekly patterns, (3) exceedance days vs NAAQS, (4) PM2.5/PM10 ratio (combustion vs dust), (5) likely source attribution (vehicular, industrial, biomass, secondary aerosol), (6) NCAP target gap, (7) recommended interventions (GRAP), (8) population health risk estimate (DALY). Constraints: align with CPCB CAAQMS QA/QC norms; cite IIT Kanpur source-apportionment study; data-anchored not speculative; INR cost-effectiveness for interventions.

prompts.title: Climate Change Vulnerability Assessment
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior climate scientist at IITM Pune. Build a vulnerability assessment for a coastal Tamil Nadu district facing rising sea level + intensifying cyclones. Cover: (1) climate scenarios (RCP 4.5 / 8.5, IPCC AR6 with India downscaling), (2) exposure mapping (population, infrastructure, agriculture, fisheries), (3) sensitivity (poverty, demographics, livelihood), (4) adaptive capacity, (5) vulnerability index calculation, (6) hot-spot prioritisation, (7) adaptation options (engineered + ecosystem-based + behavioural), (8) cost-benefit analysis, (9) M&E framework. Constraints: align with NAPCC + State Action Plan; cite NIDM + IIT-M datasets; gender + caste disaggregation; participatory approach with affected communities.

prompts.title: River Water Quality Monitoring Plan
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior NRCD scientist with 10 years on Ganga Action Plan + Yamuna Action Plan. Design a quarterly water-quality monitoring plan for a 120-km stretch of Yamuna between Wazirabad and Okhla. Cover: (1) sampling stations (12, geo-tagged), (2) parameters (DO, BOD, COD, pH, faecal coliform, NO3, PO4, heavy metals — Cr, Pb, Hg, As), (3) sampling SOP (depth, time-of-day, seasonal), (4) chain-of-custody, (5) lab analysis (NABL accredited), (6) QA/QC (blanks, duplicates, spikes), (7) data reporting (CPCB format), (8) public disclosure (Yamuna dashboard), (9) anomaly response. Constraints: align with CPCB monitoring protocol + ISO 5667; cite Namami Gange real-time monitoring; INR budget realistic; community-volunteer integration.

prompts.title: Public Consultation Documentation
prompts.prompt: Act as an EIA public-consultation specialist. Document a public hearing for a thermal power plant expansion in Singrauli, MP. Cover: (1) notice publication (EIA Notification rules — Hindi + English in 2 newspapers, 30-day window), (2) hearing logistics (district HQ, multi-lingual interpreter, livestream + recording), (3) attendees (project proponent, MoEFCC, MPPCB, district admin, gram panchayats, civil-society, affected villagers), (4) presentation by proponent, (5) public concerns documented (8 categories — air, water, displacement, health, livelihood, biodiversity, jobs, royalties), (6) responses + commitments by proponent, (7) MPPCB compliance follow-up, (8) post-hearing report submitted to MoEFCC. Constraints: align with EIA Notification 2006; respect dignity; preserve audio + transcripts; proponent commitments tracked through EC condition compliance.

prompts.title: Biodiversity Field Survey Protocol
prompts.prompt: Act as a wildlife biologist with 12 years across Indian Western Ghats. Design a 3-month biodiversity field survey for a 200-sqkm forest area in Coorg. Cover: (1) study design (transect + point-count + camera-trap + acoustic), (2) species groups (mammals, birds, herps, butterflies, plants), (3) sampling intensity (n samples + replicates), (4) team composition (4 biologists, 6 assistants, local trackers), (5) gear list (GPS, range finder, cameras, recorders), (6) data sheet templates, (7) ethics + safety, (8) data analysis (occupancy modelling, species accumulation, Chao estimator), (9) reporting + repository upload (GBIF-compatible). Constraints: align with WPA 1972 permits + state forest dept NOC; respect tribal community Forest Rights Act; data-sharing via India Biodiversity Portal.

prompts.title: Sustainability Report (BRSR) Preparation
prompts.prompt: Act as a sustainability reporting consultant with 8 years for SEBI Top 1000 listed companies. Help an Indian manufacturing company prepare its BRSR Core (Business Responsibility & Sustainability Report) for FY26. Cover: (1) NGRBC 9 principles mapping, (2) material topics + stakeholder consultation, (3) KPI gathering across E (energy, GHG Scope 1-3, water, waste, biodiversity), S (employees, communities, customers, suppliers), G (board, ethics, compliance, transparency), (4) data quality + assurance (limited / reasonable), (5) GRI / TCFD / IFRS S1-S2 alignment, (6) disclosures to SEBI BRSR portal + annual report, (7) integrated reporting framework. Constraints: align with SEBI LODR 2024 (BRSR Core mandatory FY26); respect SEBI confidentiality of competitive info; honest about gaps.

prompts.title: Carbon Footprint of Indian SME
prompts.prompt: Act as a GHG accounting consultant. Compute Scope 1 + Scope 2 + Scope 3 emissions for an Indian SME (annual turnover ₹85 Cr, manufacturing). Build a simple carbon-footprint calculator covering: (1) Scope 1 (stationary + mobile + fugitive), (2) Scope 2 (electricity from grid + RE PPA), (3) Scope 3 (purchased goods + freight + business travel + employee commute + product end-of-life), (4) emission factors (DEFRA + India CEA grid-mix), (5) data quality tiers, (6) reduction roadmap (rooftop solar, EV transition, supplier engagement), (7) offset strategy (verified carbon credits — Verra, Gold Standard, NABARD-aligned), (8) MSME climate-finance options (SIDBI). Constraints: align with GHG Protocol; quarterly reporting cadence; CSR + DPDP-aligned.

prompts.title: Solid Waste Management Plan for City
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior solid-waste management expert. Design a 5-year integrated SWM plan for a Tier-2 Indian city (population 8 lakh, ULB). Cover: (1) current state (waste generation 280 TPD, segregation 38%, disposal — open dump), (2) targets (SBM 2.0 + AMRUT 2.0), (3) source-segregation (3-bin model), (4) collection + transportation (e-rickshaw, GPS, RFID), (5) processing (composting, biomethanation, MRF), (6) sanitary landfill engineering (lining, leachate, gas), (7) dump-site remediation (legacy waste), (8) waste-pickers integration (livelihood + dignity), (9) financial model (user charge + tipping fee), (10) IEC + behaviour change. Constraints: align with SWM Rules 2016 + amendments; SBM 2.0 funding; PPP option for processing.

prompts.title: GIS Mapping for Land-Use Change
prompts.prompt: Act as a GIS analyst with 9 years on Indian land-use studies. Design a GIS mapping pipeline to track 20-year land-use change in Bengaluru urban district. Cover: (1) data sources (Landsat 5/7/8/9, Sentinel-2, IRS-1C — Bhuvan / USGS), (2) classification scheme (Anderson Level 2 + Indian-specific), (3) preprocessing (atmospheric correction, cloud-mask, georef), (4) classification approach (random forest in Google Earth Engine), (5) accuracy assessment (kappa, confusion matrix), (6) change detection (post-classification + IF-then), (7) drivers analysis (population, IT industry, infrastructure), (8) policy implication, (9) deliverables (web map + report). Constraints: align with NRSC Bhuvan + ISRO open-data; cite IIRS Dehradun training; reproducible code on GitHub.

prompts.title: Renewable Energy Site Suitability
prompts.prompt: Act as a renewable energy planner with 10 years across MNRE projects. Build a multi-criteria site suitability analysis for a 100 MW solar park in Rajasthan. Cover: (1) criteria (solar irradiance via NIWE, land availability, slope <5%, distance to substation, road access, biodiversity exclusions, archaeological exclusions, panchayat land status), (2) GIS-AHP weighting, (3) constraint mask, (4) candidate site ranking, (5) due diligence (land record, water for cleaning, transmission corridor), (6) PPA + tariff trajectory, (7) financial model (LCOE), (8) DPR outline. Constraints: align with MNRE / SECI tariff bidding; cite Bhuvan Solar Atlas; respect Banni grasslands biodiversity (if Kutch); INR Cr budgeting.

prompts.title: Plastic Waste Audit at Beach
prompts.prompt: Act as a marine debris researcher (NIO Goa school). Design a plastic-waste audit protocol at a Goa beach (Calangute, 800m stretch). Cover: (1) sampling design (random transects + grid), (2) gear (1m² quadrat, sieve, GPS, camera), (3) classification (NOAA + Indian + IS-9000 — bottles, bags, microplastics, foam, fishing gear), (4) volunteer coordination (50 volunteers + Goa beach guards), (5) sample processing (lab quantification + microplastic FTIR), (6) data entry + dashboard, (7) source attribution (local + ocean drift), (8) intervention recommendations (EPR brand-audit, refuse program), (9) IEC + community-based clean-up. Constraints: align with CPCB plastic waste rules + EPR; coastal regulation zone (CRZ) compliant; volunteer safety; respect tourism economy.

prompts.title: Climate Litigation Memo for NGT
prompts.prompt: Act as an environmental lawyer. Draft a 1,500-word factual memo to support a climate-litigation petition before the National Green Tribunal (NGT). Subject: industrial pollution affecting health of villagers near a ferroalloy plant in Odisha. Cover: (1) factual matrix (plant operations, pollution incidents 2018-2025), (2) statutory violations (Air Act, Water Act, EPA, CPCB consent conditions), (3) precedent cases (Vellore Citizens, T.N. Godavarman, M.C. Mehta line), (4) scientific evidence (independent air + water + soil + epidemiology), (5) reliefs sought (closure, remediation, victim compensation), (6) interim prayer, (7) party list + jurisdiction. Constraints: align with NGT Act 2010; cite scientific reports (CPCB, NEERI, AIIMS); maintain public-interest framing; preserve villager-respondent dignity.

prompts.title: Forest Carbon Stock Estimation
prompts.prompt: Act as a forest ecologist with 10 years across India's REDD+ pilots. Design a forest carbon-stock estimation for a 5,000-hectare reserve forest in Uttarakhand. Cover: (1) sampling design (stratified random plots — 0.1 ha circular, n=80), (2) measurements (DBH, height, species, dead wood, soil organic carbon), (3) allometric equations (Indian-FSI species-specific), (4) biomass-to-carbon conversion (0.47 IPCC default), (5) error analysis + uncertainty propagation, (6) repeat-survey plan (5-year interval), (7) MRV protocol for carbon credit (Verra VM0007), (8) community participation (joint forest management). Constraints: align with FSI India State of Forest Report; UNFCCC + IPCC AR6 guidance; respect Forest Rights Act communities; data archived on India MRV portal.

prompts.title: Policy Brief on Plastic Phase-Out
prompts.prompt: Act as a policy analyst at TERI with 8 years on Indian plastic policy. Write a 2-page policy brief for MoEFCC on phasing out additional single-use plastic items by 2027. Cover: (1) current status (2022 ban + EPR enforcement gap), (2) global benchmarks (EU SUP Directive, Australia, Indonesia), (3) Indian sectoral impact (FMCG, food delivery, retail, MSMEs), (4) substitution alternatives + cost gap, (5) MSME transition support (₹ Cr, retraining, technology), (6) enforcement (CPCB-SPCB capacity, IT-portal-driven EPR), (7) consumer behaviour change campaign, (8) recommended timeline + milestones. Constraints: align with PWM Rules 2016 + amendments; cite NITI Aayog Plastic Circular Economy report; respect informal-sector waste-pickers; INR transition cost honest.
